summary: Tested all 4 tasks: (1) Spread editor loads with real background images - PASS. (2) Font Family dropdown with system+custom fonts - PASS. (3) Letter Spacing and Line Height controls - PASS, values persist after save. (4) /api/admin/personalization/process endpoint - MISSING (404). Backend spread_blocks processing in personalization_processor.py is implemented but no admin API endpoint to test it directly.

critical.issue: Endpoint does not exist (returns 404). Task 4 requires this endpoint for testing spread_blocks PDF rendering. personalization_processor.py has _overlay_spread_blocks() but no admin-facing route to trigger it.

issues: Console warning: 'alphabetical' is not a valid enum value of type CanvasTextBaseline - repeated for every spread load. Minor Fabric.js/canvas textBaseline issue, non-blocking.

action_items: TASK 4 INCOMPLETE: Create POST /api/admin/personalization/process endpoint in server.py that accepts template_id + field_values, calls personalization_processor._overlay_spread_blocks() with spread_blocks from template, and returns output PDF path or blob. This is needed to verify spread_blocks wire into final PDF rendering.
Fix canvas textBaseline 'alphabetical' warning (minor - Fabric.js IText uses 'alphabetical' which is non-standard, use 'alphabetic' instead)
critical_code_review_comments: personalization_processor.py has letter_spacing and line_height support in _overlay_spread_blocks() but the admin API has no direct test endpoint - main flow works via order webhook background task only
Fabric.js is using 'alphabetical' as textBaseline which is not a valid CanvasRenderingContext2D enum - should be 'alphabetic'

success_rate.backend: 75% (3/4 tasks - spread images OK, fonts OK, spreads list with image_url OK; process endpoint missing)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (font dropdown with system+custom, letter_spacing, line_height, save persistence all working)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Tasks 1-3 fully verified and working. Task 4 (POST /api/admin/personalization/process) is missing. personalization_processor.py has full spread_blocks PDF rendering via PyMuPDF (fitz) with letter_spacing and line_height support. The admin API endpoint needs to be created to expose this for direct testing. Template 414c7edb-e024-4791-be25-99f17f44d3e6 now has 2 blocks on spread_0 (1 existing 'Dear [dad_name]' + 1 new test block with letter_spacing=200).
